resources.string: Scan food labels or search additives; quickly check halal status, risk level, and health notes.
Food Label Detective main header. Scan food labels or search additives to quickly see halal status, risk level, and health notes.
Server data was updated successfully at %1$s.
Server data was checked at %1$s. Your local database is already up to date.
The app is using the local database. Last successful update: %1$s.
The app is using the local database. No successful server update has been recorded yet.
The live update service is not available. The app is using the local database on this device.
Could not update the additive database. The app is using the local database. Details: %1$s
The additive database update was skipped. The local database was kept safe. Details: %1$s
About this app and usage guide
How does Food Label Detective work?

Food Label Detective is a practical label-reading tool that helps you understand food additives and E-codes found on packaged foods. You can scan a product label, search codes such as E330, E120, or E160A, or type an additive name to see a short description, purpose, halal status, risk level, and health note when available.

Scan a product label with the camera or choose a label photo from the gallery. You can also type an E-code or additive name into the search box. Examples include E330, E120, E160A, or citric acid. OCR is not perfect; lighting, blur, angle, and package reflections may affect the result.
What do Online, Local, and Offline mean?

Online: The app reached the server and checked the latest data. Local: The last successful update was recent, so the app is using the database saved on your device. Offline: The internet or server could not be reached; the app continues working with the local database on your device.
Where does the data come from?

The app updates additive records from a live server database at scheduled intervals. After a successful update, the data is also stored on your device so basic searches can continue without internet access. When the server data changes, the app tries to synchronize the local database during the next update check.

Label images captured with the camera or selected from the gallery are used only for on-device OCR processing; the app does not upload these images to a server. Internet access is used only to update the additive database and check connection status.
Version: %1$s\nAuthor: Mehmet Büküm\nYour feedback helps improve the additive database, OCR flow, and app experience.
No email app was found. The feedback email address was copied.
No browser app was found. The privacy policy link was copied.

This app is for general information only. The information shown in the app does not replace expert guidance, product labels, manufacturer statements, official notices, or certification decisions. Before making a final decision, check the product package, manufacturer information, and qualified expert guidance when needed.
Check a food label in seconds
Scan a package label or type an additive code. Examples: E330, E120, E621.
No additive was found for this code. Close matches, if available, appear below the search box.
Search by E-code or additive name
Additive search area. You can enter codes such as E330, E120, or E160A.
No health note is available for this additive.
How is the risk level estimated?

The signal bars summarize the general health note, warning text, source sensitivity, and available database classification for this additive. It is a practical guide only; it is not medical advice, certification, or a final legal decision. Always check the product label and qualified guidance when needed.
%1$s is generally used as %2$s. Its halal status is marked as %3$s and its risk level as %4$s.
The operation could not be completed
The camera could not be started.
The photo was too large to process. Please try again with a closer and clearer label photo.
The photo could not be processed.
The photo could not be captured.
This information is for general guidance only; check the product label and qualified guidance before final decisions.
Review the detected E-codes and database matches below.
OCR result: %1$d code(s) detected, %2$d found in the database.
These codes may have been read by OCR, but no database match was found. You can also try typing the code from the package manually.
Text was read from the photo, but no additive code such as E100, E160A, or E330 was found.
No readable text could be extracted from the photo. Lighting, blur, or angle may have made OCR difficult.

• Take the label photo closer and from a straight angle.
• Reduce glare and shadows.
• Make sure the text is not blurry.
• Center the visible additive-code area in the photo.
• Example codes: E330, E160A, E621
Tip: Since OCR can read text, manually typing the code into the search box may also be faster.
Camera permission is required to scan the label.
Camera permission is required to read additive codes on product labels. Photos are used only for on-device OCR processing.
Camera permission was not granted. You can enable it to scan labels or use the Gallery option.
Align the ingredients text inside this frame
Point the ingredients list at the frame. OCR runs every few seconds while live scan is on.
Choose a label photo from the gallery
